title: Accessible Data Table
description:
Build an **accessible sortable data table** with proper semantics, screen reader announcements, and keyboard interaction.

### Requirements

- Use semantic `<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<th>`, `<td>` elements
- Table has a `<caption>` for its accessible name
- Sortable column headers have `aria-sort` (`ascending`, `descending`, or `none`)
- Clicking a column header cycles: none → ascending → descending → none
- Sort state change is announced via an `aria-live="polite"` region
- Sortable headers are buttons or have `role="button"` + keyboard support
- Row selection via checkbox with `aria-label` describing the row
- A "Select All" checkbox in the header controls all rows
- Selected count is displayed and announced
- Table is wrapped in a `role="region"` with `aria-label` and `tabIndex={0}` for keyboard scrolling

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Table | `data-table` |
| Sort button (column) | `sort-{column}` |
| Select all checkbox | `select-all` |
| Row checkbox | `select-row-{id}` |
| Selected count | `selected-count` |
| Sort announcement | `sort-announcement` |
constraints:
- Must use semantic HTML table elements (no div-based tables)
- Column headers must communicate sort state via aria-sort
- Selection changes must be announced to screen readers
- Select-all must handle indeterminate state
- Table region must be keyboard-scrollable
hints: Use <th scope='col'> for column headers and <th scope='row'> for row headers to establish associations.
For indeterminate checkbox state, use a ref and set `checkbox.indeterminate = true` when some but not all rows are selected.
Keep the sort announcement region always in the DOM with empty text; update it to trigger the live region announcement.
